items.descriptionHtml:
About Porter

Porter is India's largest intra-city and inter-city logistics platform, connecting millions of customers with a trusted network of driver-partners. We are building technology-first products that redefine logistics, making it faster, smarter, and more reliable for businesses and consumers alike.


About the Role

We are looking for an experienced Product Manager to own a full product charter at Porter. You will be accountable for the charter's OKRs, from problem discovery and roadmap planning through delivery, iteration, and business impact.

You are expected to operate with high independence, run multiple concurrent discovery initiatives, manage a 2–3 quarter roadmap, and actively drive cross-functional teams to deliver outcomes.


Key Responsibilities

  • Own the roadmap and OKRs for a full product charter (2–3 quarters ahead)
  • Create detailed PRDs with problem statements, requirements, launch milestones, instrumentation, and impact sizing
  • Design and execute multiple concurrent problem and solution discovery initiatives; synthesize insights from diverse data sources and research methods
  • Build systematic frameworks to validate and refine solution options across the charter
  • Drive cross-functional execution with engineering, design, business, and data teams; proactively resolve delivery roadblocks
  • Establish a metrics-driven approach for monitoring delivery efficiency and product impact
  • Coach junior PMs on discovery techniques, PRD quality, and delivery best practices
  • Lead go-to-market planning and support operational readiness for product launches


What We Are Looking For

Must Have

  • 4–7 years of product management experience, with at least 2 years on B2C consumer-facing products at meaningful scale
  • Proven ability to own a full product charter with measurable OKR accountability
  • Strong product discovery skills: ability to synthesise insights from multiple research methods and data sources simultaneously
  • Excellent data fluency: sharp metric definitions, impact sizing, data-informed prioritisation, and anomaly-to-action capability
  • Experience with roadmap planning and backlog management (2–3 quarter horizon)
  • Ability to influence without authority across engineering, design, and business teams
  • Structured, clear communication with stakeholders across levels
  • Experience with A/B experimentation and iterative product development

Good to Have

  • Experience on platform, growth, engagement, or personalisation
  • Prior mentoring of junior PMs or cross-functional team members
  • Background at a product-led B2C company with scale (>1Mn MAU)

items.descriptionHtml:
We are looking for a Product Manager – Supply Chain to drive product strategy and execution across Licious’ farm-to-fork ecosystem. This role will work across sourcing, procurement, demand planning, inventory, warehousing, and logistics systems to build scalable, data-driven solutions.

You will own end-to-end supply chain product initiatives that improve service levels, reduce wastage, and optimize fulfillment costs, while enabling scalable growth. Working closely with business, engineering, and operations teams, you will solve complex supply chain challenges in a fast-paced D2C environment.


Key Responsibilities

  • Product Strategy & OwnershipDefine and drive product vision, roadmap, and execution across supply chain domains such as procurement, demand planning, inventory management, warehouse management, and last-mile logistics
  • Own end-to-end product lifecycle from discovery to delivery and iteration
  • Problem Solving & ExecutionIdentify inefficiencies and bottlenecks in supply chain processes and translate them into scalable tech solutions
  • Break down ambiguous problems into structured product requirements and prioritize based on impact
  • Stakeholder ManagementCollaborate with cross-functional teams including Operations, Category, Finance, and Engineering
  • Act as a bridge between business and technology teams to ensure alignment and timely delivery
  • Data-Driven Decision MakingLeverage data to improve forecasting accuracy, inventory planning, and operational efficiency
  • Define, track, and optimize key supply chain metrics (fill rate, wastage, SLA adherence, inventory turns, etc.)
  • Execution & DeliveryWork closely with engineering teams in an agile setup to deliver high-quality, scalable products
  • Drive sprint planning, backlog prioritization, and release management
  • Business & Customer ImpactEnsure product solutions improve both customer experience (availability, freshness, delivery timelines) and operational cost efficiency


Required Qualifications

  • 2–5 years of experience in Product Management, preferably in supply chain, logistics, e-commerce, or D2C startups
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a structured approach
  • Hands-on experience working with data (SQL, Excel, dashboards, or analytics tools)
  • Ability to influence cross-functional stakeholders without formal authority
  • Good understanding of product development lifecycle and agile methodologies


Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in supply chain domains such as inventory management, demand forecasting, warehouse systems, or logistics tech
  • Exposure to high-growth startup environments
  • Basic understanding of system design, APIs, and integrations
  • MBA or equivalent experience is a plus


What Success Looks Like

  • Improved supply chain efficiency through reduced wastage and better fill rates
  • Scalable and reliable systems supporting procurement, inventory, and logistics operations
  • Enhanced visibility and faster decision-making through data-driven products

items.descriptionHtml:
As a Senior Product Manager – Supply Chain Management, you will own and evolve the technology platforms and workflows that power Licious’ end-to-end supply chain. You will work closely with Supply Chain, Operations, Data, Engineering, and Finance teams to build products that improve freshness, reduce waste, and enable scalable growth.

This role requires strong product judgment, comfort with operational complexity, and the ability to balance speed, reliability, and long-term platform thinking.



What makes the role meaty...


Product Strategy & Ownership

  • Own the product vision and roadmap for supply chain systems across demand planning, procurement, production planning, warehousing, inventory, and logistics
  • Translate business goals (freshness, fill rate, cost, SLA adherence) into clear product outcomes and success metrics
  • Identify automation and digitization opportunities across supply chain workflows

Execution & Delivery

  • Lead discovery, prioritization, and delivery of features across internal platforms
  • Write high-quality PRDs, define user journeys, and ensure on-time execution with engineering and design teams
  • Drive experimentation and continuous improvement using data and operational feedback

Demand, Inventory & Planning

  • Build products that improve forecast accuracy, replenishment, and inventory turns
  • Partner with Data Science teams on forecasting models and decision-support tools
  • Enable real-time visibility into inventory, wastage, and capacity across nodes

Operations & Reliability

  • Design systems that ensure traceability, compliance, and quality control
  • Improve exception management, alerts, and operational dashboards
  • Balance system reliability with rapid iteration in a 24x7 operational environment

Stakeholder Management

  • Act as the product SPOC for Supply Chain and Operations leadership
  • Collaborate with Finance, Category, and Retail teams on cost, margins, and availability
  • Influence roadmap trade-offs by balancing operational needs and tech scalability


We hope that you have..


Experience

  • 6–10 years of product management experience, preferably in Supply Chain, Logistics, Manufacturing, Retail Ops, or Food/FMCG
  • Hands-on experience building internal platforms or operational products
  • Proven track record of shipping complex products in high-scale environments

Skills & Capabilities

  • Strong problem-solving and structured thinking
  • Data-driven mindset; comfort working with metrics, dashboards, and models
  • Ability to work with ambiguity and evolving operational constraints
  • Excellent stakeholder communication and influence skills

Good to Have

  • Experience in fresh food, cold chain, or perishable supply chains
  • Exposure to demand forecasting, inventory optimization, or WMS/TMS systems
  • Experience working with data science and optimization problems

items.descriptionHtml:
About The Team

Are you excited by the idea of building the invisible engines that power great products? The Platform Product team at slice works on internal systems, tooling, workflows, and scalable infrastructure that enable teams across the company to move faster and build better experiences.

We solve complex operational and platform problems across customer support, payments, risk, operations, and internal product ecosystems. Think of us as the circuitry behind the neon sign ⚡

Analytical, systems-driven, deeply collaborative, and relentlessly curious, our team thrives on creating leverage through technology.


About The Role

slice is looking for a Platform Product Manager who is passionate about building scalable internal products and operational platforms. In this role, you will work closely with engineering, operations, support, analytics, and business teams to create systems that improve efficiency, automation, visibility, and decision-making across the organization.

If you enjoy solving workflow inefficiencies, designing scalable platforms, and building products that empower internal teams, we would love to meet you.


What You Will Do

  • Understand pain points, workflows, and operational challenges across internal stakeholders including Operations, Support, Risk, Finance, and Business teams.
  • Build scalable internal platforms, tooling, and automation systems that improve productivity and operational efficiency.
  • Define and prioritize platform roadmaps aligned with business goals and long-term architectural vision.
  • Work closely with leadership, engineering, design, analytics, and operations teams to drive platform adoption and execution.
  • Identify opportunities to reduce manual effort through workflow optimization, integrations, and intelligent automation.
  • Own end-to-end product lifecycle from problem discovery and requirement gathering to launch and iteration.
  • Establish metrics to measure platform effectiveness, operational impact, and stakeholder satisfaction.
  • Balance short-term operational needs with long-term platform scalability and reliability.
  • Drive continuous improvement through fast iterations, experimentation, and stakeholder feedback loops.


What You Will Need

  • 2–4 years of Product Management experience in platform products, internal tooling, fintech, SaaS, or fast-paced startup environments.
  • Strong problem-solving mindset with the ability to navigate ambiguous and complex operational challenges.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to work across technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Experience collaborating closely with engineering, analytics, operations, and design teams.
  • Ability to convert fragmented requirements into structured product plans and execution roadmaps.
  • Strong understanding of product thinking, prioritization frameworks, and data-driven decision-making.
  • Bias for action with an ownership mindset and attention to detail.
  • Familiarity with workflow automation, platform ecosystems, APIs, or internal operations systems is a plus.


Life at slice


Life so good, you’d think we’re kidding:

  • Competitive salaries. Period.
  • An extensive medical insurance that looks out for our employees & their dependents. We’ll love you and take care of you, our promise.
  • Flexible working hours. Just don’t call us at 3AM, we like our sleep schedule.
  • Tailored vacation & leave policies so that you enjoy every important moment in your life.
  • A reward system that celebrates hard work and milestones throughout the year. Expect a gift coming your way anytime you kill it here.
  • Learning and upskilling opportunities. Seriously, not kidding.
  • Good food, games, and a cool office to make you feel like home. An environment so good, you’ll forget the term “colleagues can’t be your friends”.


About us

slice

A new bank for a new India


slice’s purpose is to make the world better at using money and time, with a major focus on building the best consumer experience for your money. We’ve all felt how slow, confusing, and complicated banking can be. So, we’re reimagining it. We’re building every product from scratch to be fast, transparent, and feel good, because we believe that the best products transcend demographics, like how great music touches most of us.


Our cornerstone products and services: slice savings account, slice UPI credit card, slice UPI, and slice business are designed to be simple, rewarding, and completely in your control. At slice, you’ll get to build things you’d use yourself and shape the future of banking in India. We tailor our working experience with the belief that the present moment is the only real thing in life. And we have harmony in the present the most when we feel happy and successful together.


We’re backed by some of the world’s leading investors, including Tiger Global, Insight Partners, Advent International, Blume Ventures, and Gunosy Capital.

items.descriptionHtml:
Product Manager | Urban Company


About the Company


Urban Company is a technology platform offering customers a variety of services at home. Customers use our platform to book services such as beauty treatments, haircuts, massage therapy, cleaning, plumbing, carpentry, appliance repair, painting, etc., all delivered in the comfort of their home and at a time of their choosing. We promise our customers a high-quality, standardized, and reliable service experience. To fulfill this promise, we work closely with our hand-picked service partners, enabling them with technology, training, products, tools, financing, insurance, and brand, helping them succeed and deliver on this promise.


Urban Company started as UrbanClap in November 2014, when its founders, Abhiraj, Raghav, and Varun, realized that the home services industry was predominantly unorganized, fragmented, and offline. The founders felt that the industry was ripe for disruption, and launched Urban Company, with three key principles in mind:

  1. Customer love: Build a platform that offers truly delightful and differentiated services
  2. Partner empowerment: Build a deep, full-stack partnership with service partners, helping improve their earnings and overall livelihood
  3. Technology first: Bring innovation and technology to an age-old industry


About the Role & Team


The Product Management team creates the vision to empower millions of service professionals across the world to deliver service experiences at home like never seen before. Our mission is to build a full-stack platform that enables these professionals to become micro-entrepreneurs, organizing them and multiplying their earnings.


You will work on mission-critical pillars of our "Partner Empowerment Stack" such as

  • Market Access & Business Growth: Building systems that drive a 2-3x jump in partner earnings by connecting them with high-demand market access and brand equi
  • Standardization: Defining and implementing pricing, deliverables, Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), delivery tracking, and payment systems to ensure consistency
  • Training & Upskilling: Developing product capabilities for upfront and refresher classroom training programs and certification
  • Financing & Insurance: Creating financial products that facilitate bank account opening, loans, insurance, and other benefits for partners
  • Product & Tools Procurement: Managing the supply chain product stack for consumables, spares, cosmetics, cleaning materials, and paints
  • Global Scalability: Contributing to our new design system, "Align DS," which acts as the torchbearer for UC as it steps into global markets


Responsibilities:


  • Drive Product Vision: Think bold and big to define long-term answers rather than short-term hacks, owning the roadmap for your vertical
  • Customer Empathy: Talk directly to customers to truly understand their problems, empathizing with them to derive deep insights
  • Data-Driven Execution: Ground every decision in data and the voice of the customer, never assuming the answer without validation
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Communicate and work collaboratively with engineering, design, and business teams to ship groundbreaking and innovative products
  • Quality Assurance: Maintain a high bar on everything you touch, caring deeply about your product and giving your stamp of approval only when it meets excellence standards


What we need:


  • Customer Obsession: You get energy from interacting with customers and understanding their core problems
  • Analytical Mindset: You do not assume answers; you rely on data and customer feedback to guide your decisions
  • Strategic Thinking: Ability to think about the long-term picture and avoid short-term fixes
  • High Ownership: A go-getter attitude with a willingness to be hands-on and a high standard for quality
  • Collaborative Skills: Strong ability to communicate and work effectively with diverse teams


What can you expect


  • Impact: A phenomenal work environment with massive, direct impact on the lives of people
  • High-Performance Culture: A high-velocity environment at the cutting edge of growth, where you will rub shoulders with some of the smartest folks in the ecosystem
  • Ownership & Autonomy: A culture of 'doers' rather than 'approvals,' offering strong ownership expectations and the freedom to fail
  • Significant Value: Opportunity to participate in our ESOP program, which has generated significant value for employees through buyback programs.


At Urban Company we are committed to providing equal and fair opportunities in employment and creating an inclusive work environment. We endeavor to maintain a work environment free from harassment based on age, colour, physical ability, marital status, parental status, ethnic origin, religion, sexual orientation, or gender identity.

items.descriptionHtml:
Product Manager - AI Recruitment, Assessment & EOR Platform

Location: Bengaluru

Experience: 4–8 years


About Wisemonk

We're building the world's most trusted India-EOR and global hiring platform — and now, a native AI layer on top of it. Recruitment, assessment, onboarding, payroll, compliance, benefits, devices: all the work it takes to hire and run a team in India, increasingly run by agents instead of humans pushing tickets. We serve 300+ global companies, manage $20M+ in payroll, and rate 4.8/5 on G2. We move fast, own outcomes end-to-end, and don't tolerate red tape — internally or externally.


Role Overview:

You'll own the product surface where AI meets the messy reality of hiring across borders — sourcing, screening, structured assessments, EOR onboarding, and the agent workflows that string them together. This is not a roadmap-grooming role. You'll be in the trenches with engineering, design, GTM, and ops, shipping weekly, talking to customers daily, and making the calls on what we build, what we don't, and what we kill.


What you'll do:

  • Own a full product area end-to-end — from problem framing and customer discovery through specs, launch, adoption, and iteration.
  • Define and ship the AI-native experience for recruitment and assessment: agentic sourcing, candidate evaluation, interview workflows, and the loops that make the system get smarter over time.
  • Partner with engineering on agent design — tool use, evals, guardrails, and where humans stay in the loop vs. where they get out of the way.
  • Work directly with customers (founders, HR heads, talent leaders at fast-growing startups) to pressure-test what we're building.
  • Bring rigor to prioritization — what moves the needle, what's a distraction, and ruthless follow-through to make sure the team ships.
  • Collaborate across GTM, ops, and compliance to make sure what we build actually lands and scales.


What we're looking for:

  • 4–8 years total experience, with 2–3+ years as a PM at a fast-paced, growth-stage startup (not just a large company rotation).
  • A track record of shipping — concrete products you owned, the problems they solved, and the numbers they moved.
  • Prior work in HR tech, recruitment tech, assessment, or workforce/payroll platforms is a strong plus.
  • Hands-on experience building with LLMs, agents, or AI-native workflows — not just using ChatGPT, but reasoning about evals, prompts, tool use, and where agents fail.
  • Strategic and intentional: you can zoom out to the 12-month bet and zoom in to the JIRA ticket without losing the thread.
  • A task master: you don't let things slip. Deadlines, follow-ups, decisions, dependencies — you own them.
  • Collaborative by default — you bring engineers, designers, and GTM along, not drag them.
  • Strong written communication. You can turn ambiguity into a one-pager the team can build from.


Nice to have:

  • Experience with EOR, global payroll, or compliance products.
  • Technical background or comfort reading code.
  • Worked on 0→1 products, not just incremental features.


Why join Wisemonk:

  • Genuinely early on a category-defining product — AI-native global hiring is being invented now, and you'll shape it.
  • Direct line to the founder and customers. Decisions get made in days, not quarters.
  • Real ownership, real impact, real upside.

items.descriptionHtml:
Who We Are

Testsigma is building the world’s first Quality Intelligence Platform — the infrastructure layer between every code change and every confident release. AI has collapsed how long it takes to build software. It has not changed how long it takes to trust it. That gap is the problem we exist to close — with autonomous agents that author the right tests, run them at the right time, heal them when the application changes, and signal whether a release is actually safe to ship.

Why This Role Exists

Test generation has been commoditized. The defensible moat has moved to autonomous testing: software that learns the customer’s application, runs the right tests at the right time, heals itself when the application changes, and gives engineering leaders a calibrated release signal. This role owns a major surface of that product end-to-end.

What You Own

Two things above everything: what gets built, and whether customers actually use it to make a decision. Ownership areas include:

  • Context and signal infrastructure — ingesting PRDs, code, analytics, and support signals into a compounding knowledge layer
  • Agent surfaces — AI agents that author, plan, execute, heal, and analyze tests, with human-in-the-loop touchpoints
  • Coverage, traceability, and release intelligence — requirement-to-test mapping and release-readiness signals
  • SDLC and developer experience — authoring inside AI-native dev tools, Skills/MCP/CLI distribution
  • Executive reporting — dashboards a VP Engineering can put in a board update
  • Platform foundations — onboarding, integrations (Jira, GitHub, Confluence, Linear, Mixpanel, and more), and cross-cutting work that turns capability into a deployable product

What You’ll Build in Year One

  • Intake flow: new customer connects Jira, GitHub, and one analytics source in <30 min, sees first useful output in <1 week
  • Coverage view an engineering manager uses during sprint reviews to make real decisions
  • Release-readiness score a VP Engineering trusts enough for a board update
  • Agent surfaces customers let run on production-adjacent systems — measured by autonomy rate
  • Skills + MCP + CLI distribution surface with at least one AI-native dev tool as a launch partner
  • Migration path from legacy suites (Selenium, Playwright, previous Testsigma TMS) without throwing away existing tests

Non-Negotiable

  • 4–9 years total experience, minimum 3 years in product management. Engineer-turned-PM strongly preferred.
  • Hands-on AI-native execution in the last 12 months — you’ve built something with Claude, Cursor, LangChain, or comparable tools and can describe specifically what broke.
  • You generate your own prototypes: wireframes, clickable prototypes, or first-cut code.
  • You can sit with engineers, read a PR, whiteboard a state machine, and pair-debug a flaky test.
  • You’ve owned a 0-to-1 product to GA — a thing that didn’t exist before you, that customers now use.
  • You’ve had direct, hard customer conversations: renewals, escalations, churn calls.
  • You write clearly without an LLM polishing it. The take-home will test this.
  • 5 days in Koramangala office; comfortable operating across time zones for an 80%-NA customer base.
  • Looking for 3–5 years of stability. Tenure pattern is checked; exits under 18 months are probed.

Nice-to-Haves

Prior testing/QA/developer tools experience; Selenium or Playwright familiarity including failure modes; knowledge graph vs. RAG architecture understanding; experience moving a product from QA-buyer to engineering-leadership positioning; LLM-as-judge or eval harness design; founder or early-stage product experience.

What Strong Looks Like

  • Takes a vague directive and returns a sequenced roadmap in a week with customer interviews, a flow sketch, a draft schema, and explicit asks — not a request for more meetings.
  • Runs toward where the decision is being made, not away from it.
  • Ships rough things on Friday and learns on Monday rather than shipping perfect things in eight weeks.
  • Treats GTM as a peer function — writes enablement, joins sales calls, brings a positioning POV with conviction.
  • Has taste about what not to build and will defend killing a feature under pressure.
  • Reframes the conversation to CTOs and VP Engineering on release confidence, not QA managers on test authoring speed.

What This Role Is Not

Not a PRD-writing, backlog-grooming, or strategy-deck role. Not research-to-quarterly-report. Not hybrid or remote. You execute the strategy alongside engineering, generate the first design cut, and own the full surface end-to-end.

items.descriptionHtml:
About the Company

Ema is building the next generation AI technology to empower every employee in the enterprise to be their most creative and productive. Our proprietary tech allows enterprises to delegate most repetitive tasks to Ema, the Universal AI employee. We are founded by ex-Google, Coinbase, Okta executives, and serial entrepreneurs. We’re well-funded by the top investors and angels in the world. Ema is based in Silicon Valley with offices in Bangalore. This will be a hybrid role where we expect employees to work from the office three days a week.


About the Role

In this role, your work will involve:

Product Strategy & Vision:

  • Define and communicate the product strategy and roadmap based on market trends, customer needs, and company goals.
  • Work closely with senior management and stakeholders to align the product strategy with business objectives.

Market & User Research:

  • Conduct market research and competitor analysis to identify opportunities, gaps, and market demands.
  • Understand customer needs and translate them into product requirements.
  • Define user personas and use cases to ensure products meet user expectations.

Product Development:

  • Collaborate with engineering, design, and marketing teams to deliver products that are functional, user-friendly, and aligned with business goals.
  • Prioritize product features, enhancements, and bug fixes based on customer feedback, technical feasibility, and business impact.
  • Manage the product development lifecycle, including setting timelines and milestones.

Cross-Functional Collaboration:

  • Work closely with cross-functional teams (sales, marketing, customer support) to ensure successful product launches.
  • Communicate progress, roadblocks, and product updates with stakeholders and teams across the organization.
  • Lead and facilitate communication between technical and non-technical teams.

Performance Tracking & Iteration:

  • Define key performance indicators (KPIs) for product success.
  • Track product performance post-launch and gather customer feedback for continuous improvement.
  • Iterate on product features based on data insights and feedback.

Stakeholder Management:

  • Present product updates, roadmaps, and strategies to senior leadership and key stakeholders.
  • Manage relationships with external vendors and partners as needed.

Go-to-Market Strategy:

  • Collaborate with the marketing and sales teams to define go-to-market strategies for product launches.
  • Ensure proper communication and training for internal teams and customers during product releases.


Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Computer Science, or a related field. An MBA or equivalent experience is a plus.
  • 3-5 years of experience in product management, preferably in the tech or software industry.
  • Strong understanding of product development processes, including agile methodologies.
  • Ability to work with cross-functional teams and influence without direct authority.
  • Excellent communication, leadership, and problem-solving skills.
  • Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data and make informed decisions.
  • Strong organizational and project management skills.


Preferred Skills

  • Familiarity with tools like JIRA, Asana, or other project management platforms.
  • Knowledge of user experience (UX) design principles.
  • Experience working with APIs and technical teams.
  • Data-driven mindset with experience in A/B testing and user research methodologies.


Equal Opportunity Statement

Ema Unlimited is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, or genetics.

  • Owns the execution of the delivery of functions in support of the innovative product plan and roadmap for the Digital Banking Payments & Cash Management solution.
  • Execute against the product roadmap to drive product development and meet customer satisfaction & LOB financial objectives.
  • Oversee the product life cycle from concept through delivery.
  • Works closely with other Product Managers, the Sr. Director and Vice President of Payments & Cash Management to ensure a coherent strategy and product experience across all of the Bottomline Payments products.
  • Work with internal stakeholders and external customers to define and prioritize product requirements.

Subject matter expertise required:

The role has a specific functional area to be supported.

A specific focus of this role will be the formation and execution of the Digital Banking Payments strategy and roadmap.

Other areas of responsibility within the general Payments and Cash Management solution will also be assigned.

An additional responsibility of the role will be the participation in our customer engagement initiative, including

  • Requirement’s realization
  • Release reviews and previews (monthly)
  • Roadmap reviews quarterly
  • Early Access Sandbox Events (aka test fests)
  • Customer Advisory Forum events (quarterly and ad hoc)

Skill Set/Experience

Required Skills:

  • Minimum of 5 years’ experience as a Product Manager
  • 5+ years managing technology products and success defining and launching applications
  • Proven background in product management of innovative technology solutions, preferably in a SaaS environment. Exposure to Agile methodologies, especially SCRUM, is required.
  • Strong domain expertise in corporate payments and cash management
  • Worked in multi-product team environment
  • Demonstrated ability to meet or beat assigned targets and deadlines consistently
  • Proven ability to influence cross-functional teams without formal authority
  • Demonstrable knowledge and understanding of designing software solutions
  • Work in partnership with Sales to ensure sales staff is educated on the value statements of the product.
  • Ability to present in front of clients/prospects.
  • The ability to travel up to 5%

Preferred Skills:

  • Ability to write clear market-driven product requirements
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills and teamwork skills
  • Excellent verbal and written communications skills and outstanding interpersonal skills.
  • Self-motivated with strong problem-solving skills

Reporting

  • Reports to the Sr. Director Product Management

items.descriptionHtml:
Sumo Logic is seeking a Product Manager to define the future of our Security Operations platform. As a member of our product team, you will drive the vision and execution of Threat Detection and Response and define the intersection of AI and SecOps. Working across engineering, customer success, sales, and marketing, you will transform complex challenges into intuitive, powerful solutions that enable security teams to: detect threats faster, investigate with confidence, and respond decisively. This is an opportunity to shape products that directly impact how enterprises defend against evolving cyber threats while delivering measurable business value to customers.

What You Will Be Doing

  • Defining the vision, strategy, and roadmap for an exceptional user experience and innovative Threat Detection and Response features that drive customer engagement and satisfaction across our platform.
  • Collaborating with customers and stakeholders to gather and analyze user feedback, use cases, and requirements to ensure we meet their needs while enhancing the overall platform user experience.
  • Maintaining a detailed product roadmap supported by data analysis and competitive positioning to prioritize features and enhancements effectively.
  • Working with cross-functional teams to identify and gather platform requirements that enhance user experience and dashboard functionalities.
  • Representing the voice of the customer to product engineering teams and executives, ensuring user needs are at the forefront of product development.
  • Partnering with engineering teams to ensure our dashboarding solutions are scalable, reliable, and performant, making prioritization decisions as necessary.
  • Contributing to go-to-market strategies, collaborating with product marketing and sales to effectively communicate the value of our user experience and dashboarding capabilities.
  • Developing sales enablement materials, including whitepapers, blogs, presentations, and product demos to showcase our dashboarding features.

What You Bring

  • 3+ years of experience in product management, preferably with a focus on cybersecurity, and a proven track record of success in execution
  • Strong understanding of security operations technologies (SIEM, SOAR, XDR, Threat Intelligence)
  • Experience in working with B2B SaaS products and the ability to navigate complex decision-making environments.
  • Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with a proven track record of effectively engaging with customers and cross-functional teams.
  • A data-driven mindset, with the ability to use analytics to inform product decisions and prioritize development efforts.
  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field; an MBA is a plus.

Why Join Sumo Logic?

You will be working on one of the largest scale data problems in the industry. Your work directly impacts the company’s bottom line and determines our ability to innovate by freeing up capital for new product investments.

About Us

Sumo Logic, Inc. helps make the digital world secure, fast, and reliable by unifying critical security and operational data through its Intelligent Operations Platform. Built to address the increasing complexity of modern cybersecurity and cloud operations challenges, we empower digital teams to move from reaction to readiness—combining agentic AI-powered SIEM and log analytics into a single platform to detect, investigate, and resolve modern challenges. Customers around the world rely on Sumo Logic for trusted insights to protect against security threats, ensure reliability, and gain powerful insights into their digital environments. For more information, visit www.sumologic.com.

Sumo Logic Privacy Policy. Employees will be responsible for complying with applicable federal privacy laws and regulations, as well as organizational policies related to data protection.

items.descriptionHtml:
Who we are

DigiCert is a global leader in intelligent trust. We protect the digital world by ensuring the security, privacy, and authenticity of every interaction. Our AI-powered DigiCert ONE platform unifies PKI, DNS, and certificate lifecycle management, to secure infrastructure, software, devices, messages, AI content and agents. Learn why more than 100,000 organizations, including 90% of the Fortune 500, choose DigiCert to stop today’s threats and prepare for a quantum-safe future at www.digicert.com

 

Job summary

DigiCert is redefining the Domain Name System as an AI-native trust layer — enabling secure, intelligent, and autonomous resolution for the next era of the Internet.

As a Product Manager, you will lead innovation at the intersection of AI, DNS, and digital trust. You will collaborate with engineering, and GTM teams to build cloud-native DNS services that use inference and automation to detect anomalies, optimize performance, and strengthen security. You will also leverage AI tools to move fast — turning ideas and insights into a clear, measurable roadmap.

 

What you will do

  • Define and own the AI-driven DNS roadmap, aligned with DigiCert’s trust and cloud strategy.
  • Translate complex AI/ML capabilities into customer-focused, market-ready features.
  • Partner with engineering and MLOps teams to deliver secure, scalable, cloud-native services.
  • Use AI-assisted tools to analyze feedback, synthesize insights, and iterate quickly.
  • Define and track success metrics — adoption, latency, reliability, trust-signal accuracy.
  • Drive GTM execution with marketing and sales — launches, messaging, pricing, enablement.
  • Engage customers, partners, and standards groups to validate use cases and guide innovation.
  • Foster a data-driven product culture using telemetry, dashboards, and A/B experimentation.
  • Ship production-ready features in days, not weeks, and iterate continuously.

 

What you will have

  • 4+ years of product-management experience in cloud, networking, or infrastructure SaaS.
  • Experience working closely with engineering teams on AWS, Azure or GCP.
  • Understanding of DNS, networking, or distributed-systems fundamentals.
  • Familiarity with AI/ML systems — especially inference pipelines or MLOps workflows (desired).
  • Proven ability to deliver 0→1 products and iterate rapidly in agile environments.
  • Strong analytical and storytelling skills; fluent in data-driven decision-making.
  • Leverage AI-assisted tools (e.g., Figma Make, Cursor, vibe coding, Copilot) to:
    • Rapidly prototype workflows and UI concepts
    • Validate ideas early with engineers and customers
    • Iterate on requirements before build
  • Experience supporting or leading GTM activities (launches, collateral, pricing, adoption tracking).
  • Self-starter with an ownership mindset and a passion for 0→1 building in fast-moving environments.

 

Nice to have

  • Startup speed, enterprise scale.
  • Builders, not operators.
  • Security, trust, and automation by default.
  • AI-accelerated, open-source driven, cloud-native by design.

 

Benefits

  • Generous time off policies
  • Top shelf benefits
  • Education, wellness and lifestyle support

items.descriptionHtml:
About The Team

Product Management in Meesho closely resembles our rocketship growth. Because we obsess over understanding customer behaviour, we’ve been able to carve out an e-commerce niche in Tier 2/3/4 towns in Bharat. Our business growth is testimony to a singular focus on how we think of ‘product’ for our unique customers - ones that have barely shopped online, but are now able to because of Meesho.

Fun fact: Nearly 7% of India’s households shop with us. 😉

We are driven by a ‘user-first’ mindset. We have a strong bias for action. Above all, our team is driven by impact, not features. We drive ‘problem discovery’ & delivery - not only obsess over “what to build”, but execute with rigour to create impact.

But there’s more – we love to have fun as much as we love to work hard. From movie buffs to sportspersons, we have a super cool team. So, if a game of badminton after a day of brainstorming at work sounds like something you would love to do, then join us!

About The Role

We are looking for a Product Manager I who is passionate about solving high-impact problems in a fast-paced start-up environment with a high degree of autonomy. This is inherently a cross-functional leadership role where you will work closely with teams such as software engineering, UX design, category management, marketing, operations, finance, etc. to bring a new product to life.

What You Will Do

  • Market & Customer Understanding: Understand the e-commerce market and needs of customers/sellers deeply through landscape analysis, customer interviews, user research, competition analysis and other qualitative/quantitative techniques.
  • Problem Identification & Prioritization: Adopt a structured approach to identify what problems need to be solved and break them down into smaller chunks. Prioritize what problems need to be solved now vs. later.
  • Solution Discovery: Facilitate a process to come up with the best technology-led ideas to solve the prioritized problems. Harness the creativity of not just yourself but of other teams such as design, engineering., business, etc Be willing to experiment with multiple solution ideas and test them using MVPs, prototypes, etc.
  • Product Delivery: Define detailed product requirements and work collaboratively with a cross-functional team (design, engg. business, analytics, etc.) to bring a product to life. Ensure high product quality through processes such as user acceptance testing, dogfooding, etc.
  • Go-to-Market Planning: Create a go-to-market plan working with business/marketing stakeholders to maximize product adoption and success.
  • Adoption & Metrics: Define product metrics for your area, and track them continuously. Identify usage patterns and come up with action plans to move metrics in the right direction.
  • Roadmap Creation: Create a product roadmap for your area that has at least a 3-month forward-looking view of key customer/business problems to be solved.


What You Will Need

  • Overall experience of 3+ yrs with at least 3+ yrs in product management in a tech-led company (consumer internet space is preferred)
  • Exceptional problem-solving skills based on the first principles of thinking
  • Good understanding of technology, and comfort with product management processes such as A/B experimentation, writing product requirement documents, managing product backlogs, and creating roadmaps
  • Good communication and stakeholder management skills to get work done across different functions and drive product development

  • Experience in EdTech, SaaS, or consumer tech products
  • Exposure to product-led growth strategies

Why Join Masai?

items.descriptionHtml:
The charter: Own the suite of non-checkout products for SMEs — Payment Links, Payment Pages, and the workflows built around them. Reimagine how merchants create, manage, and automate their payment collection using agentic AI.

What we need:

  • Deep empathy for SME merchants — you understand their operational pain. Manual follow-ups, reconciliation headaches, payment reminders that never go out.
  • Agent-first thinking — your job is to design workflows where agents take away operational overhead entirely. Not "add an AI feature" — fundamentally rethink who does the work.
  • Builder mindset — you've shipped products, not just strategy decks. You care about the details: the edge case in the payment link flow, the WhatsApp reminder that fires at the wrong time.
  • Comfort with ambiguity — this product suite is being reimagined. You'll define what "No-Code payments" looks like when agents handle the complexity.


What you'll do -

  • Build Customer Empathy: PMs have to regularly meet and understand customer needs first-hand as well as stay on top of the customer pulse via secondary insights - both qualitative and quantitative.
  • Devise Strategy: Define both long-term strategy and quarterly roadmap to achieve the product vision and create impact.
  • Conceptualize new solutions: Product Conceptualization with a problem first, data-driven, and test & iterate approach. Ensures conceptualization from a platform and a long-term view. Ensures value-based prioritization of product initiatives.
  • Build products: Collaborate with Engineering and ecosystem players to build products that customers love. Produce high-quality product specifications and effectively prioritize and manage the scope of each product launch to deliver products and features on time.
  • Launch products: Draft and execute a go-to-market plan in conjunction with Product Marketing. Be the internal and external authority and advocate for your given area of focus.
  • Ecosystem collaboration: Self-starter to collaborate closely with ecosystem players to co-build products that don’t exist


Mandatory Qualifications:

  • You are obsessed with users but grounded in business and data.
  • You have 4+ years of experience in product management
  • You're hands-on, hungry, and thrive in fast-paced environments.
  • You have a strong command over product analytics and experimentation frameworks.
  • You understand technology deeply enough to work well with engineers, but your core superpower is thinking from a customer-first and business-first lens.
  • You think in systems, love solving root problems, and approach prioritization through impact and effort.
  • You’re structured in thinking, biased for action, and love taking ownership end-to-end.

items.descriptionHtml:
Greetings from Tata Consultancy Services!!!


Required Experience: 8+ Years

Location: Bangalore


About the Role:

As a Product Manager, you will play a pivotal role in bringing the magic of stories and franchises to life through our diverse range of theme parks, resorts, cruise ships, and other unique vacation experiences. This early career role is perfect for a problem-solver and independent worker who is passionate about creating innovative and customer-focused products. You will be instrumental in developing and executing product strategies that enhance the guest experience and drive business growth.


Responsibilities:

In this intermediate role, you will be responsible for solving a range of problems and analyzing possible solutions with moderate guidance and direction. You will provide informal guidance to new team members and explain complex information clearly. This role requires creativity, passion, and a customer-focused mindset to succeed.


  • Develop and execute product strategies for Experiences, including theme parks, resorts, and cruise ships.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to align, frame, and deliver the strategic vision.
  • Translate product concepts into user stories, epics, requirement documents, and test cases.
  • Utilize guest research, focus groups, and behavioral analytics for data-driven decision-making.
  • Ensure appropriate approval for new features from financial, legal, privacy, and security teams.
  • Provide informal guidance to new team members and explain complex information clearly.
  • Participate in scrum ceremonies to guide priority decisions and remove roadblocks.


Required Qualifications:

  • Strong problem-solving skills and ability to analyze possible solutions.

  • Experience in translating product concepts into user stories, epics, and requirement documents.
  • Proficiency in utilizing guest research and analytics for data-driven decision-making.
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
  • Detail-oriented and able to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
  • Bachelor's Degree.
  • 8+ years of experience in product management.


Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience in the travel, retail, or consumer industry.
  • Knowledge of Agile and Scrum methodologies.
  • Experience working with distributed teams.

items.descriptionHtml:
Revenera helps product executives build better products, accelerate time to value and monetize what matters. Revenera’s leading solutions help software and technology companies drive top line revenue with modern software monetization, understand usage and compliance with software usage analytics, empower the use of open source with software composition analysis and deliver an excellent user experience—for embedded, on premises, cloud, and SaaS products.

Revenera helps product executives build better products, accelerate time to value and monetize what matters. Revenera’s leading solutions help software and technology companies drive top line revenue with modern software monetization, understand usage and compliance with software usage analytics, and deliver an excellent user experience—for embedded, on-premises, cloud, and SaaS products.

Our software monetization platform supports licensing, entitlement management, and usage analytics and is used by leading software and technology companies to enable their business models, prevent revenue leakage, and gain insight into their software businesses. Our installation software enables safe, reliable delivery experiences, allowing development teams to focus on what matters most.

We are looking for a highly motivated, experienced Product Owner and People Manager in India to play a key role in the agile development of Software Monetization solutions.

You will manage a team of Product Owners responsible for driving feature development, product enhancements, and roadmap execution, ensuring the voice of the customer and the business is fully represented within the agile process. The team works closely with architects, product managers, marketing, and engineering to ensure business, quality, and functional objectives are met with every release.

The Product Owner team ensures that product updates align with expectations, meet delivery commitments, and provide clear visibility into progress, impact, and risks. As the team manager, you are accountable for the team’s professional development, motivation, and consistent application of agile and product best practices.

You will work closely with Strategic Product Managers to understand the defined roadmap and partner with Engineering teams to successfully deliver product increments, modelling operational excellence.

Job Description

Working across the Software Monetization product lines, help deliver value through FlexNet Operations, FlexNet Licensing, and compliance solutions.

Responsibilities

  • Lead and support the Product Owner team through regular one‑to‑one and team meetings, building confidence, ownership, and delivery maturity
  • Apply and promote agile principles and effective product ownership practices within Product Management and across Revenera
  • Track individual and team progress against goals, supporting both delivery outcomes and professional growth
  • Own the quality of epics, user stories, and acceptance criteria, while actively coaching Product Owners on backlog management and stakeholder collaboration
  • Guide roadmap elaboration to achieve clarity of scope, effort, and value, enabling informed prioritisation and trade‑off decisions
  • Identify and remove delivery impediments, escalating effectively when required
  • Support Product Management with customer insight, research, and structured feedback from internal and external stakeholders
  • Act as a trusted product representative to engineering, leadership, and support teams
  • Participate in customer interactions, particularly for validating new functionality and understanding enhancement or defect trends

Required Skills And Knowledge

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical product experience
  • Strong experience as a Product Owner or Senior Product Owner, with early people‑management or mentoring responsibility
  • Demonstrated ability to lead through influence, structure, and clarity, even when formal authority is still developing
  • Experience delivering complex SaaS or enterprise software products
  • Working knowledge of multi‑platform, on‑premises software development, including embedded or SDK‑based systems
  • Solid understanding of B2B software operating in air‑gapped, hybrid, and online environments
  • Practical experience with agile software development methodologies
  • Strong organisational, prioritisation, and decision‑making skills
  • Clear written and verbal English communication skills for working with global stakeholders
  • Willingness to collaborate across time zones, including occasional evening meetings
  • A growth mindset with the ambition to progress toward broader people‑leadership responsibility

Revenera is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will be considered for open roles regardless of age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local/national laws, policies and/or regulations. Regarding disability, we encourage candidates requiring accommodations to please let us know by emailing careers@flexera.com.

items.title: Senior Product Manager
items.description: Acceldata is reimagining the way companies observe their Data! Acceldata is the pioneer and leader in data observability, revolutionizing how enterprises manage and observe data by offering comprehensive insights into various key aspects of data, data pipelines and data infrastructure across various environments. Our platform empowers data teams to manage products effectively by ensuring data quality, preventing failures, and controlling costs.As a Senior Product ManagerYou'll play a key role in setting the near term goals and long term strategy for the whole product. You'll lead collaboration efforts with cross functional teams to deliver product success. You'll build a coherent product narrative that ties together all of the strands of a product or set of features into a coherent whole. You'll be the go-to person for all strategic and tactical concerns on the product and business area and drive decisions to resolution.A day in the life of Senior Product Manager Work with cross functional teams and key stake holders to develop product vision, roadmap and design. Work with a team of 15+ engineers to plan and execute the product roadmap. Drive the product strategy for the sub-group. Mentor the Product managers by providing deep understanding of the overall product strategy, provide high level insights from formative research, market gaps or cutting edge technical insights. Drive the broader roadmap with a view beyond the next 12 monthsYou are a great fit for this role if you have Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related field. 10+ years of experience in Product Development setup, with at least 4+ years of experience in Product Management. 3+ years of Enterprise Data Product experience as a PM. Experience in full cycle Product Management ( Ideation to Launch )- track record of delivering successful products and features, all the way through to adoption Experience leading Cross functional collaboration efforts. Deep technical understanding of the product and the complexities involved in building the product. Strong business acumen. Excellent articulation and presentation skills- ability to clearly and concisely communicate complex topics to diverse stakeholders (engineers, customers, etc.) in written and verbal form. Deep understanding of databases, data processing platforms, analytics tools and machine learning platforms. Ability to think rigorously and make hard decisions and trade-offs. Ability to work in ambiguous, fast-moving environments and have a curiosity to learn the domain at a deep level. Developing a high level of autonomy and responsibility while encouraging a healthy work environment. We care for our team Mentorship & GrowthESOPsMedical and Life InsurancePaid Maternity & Parental LeaveCorporate Uber ProgramLearning & Development Support Acceldata for All We are a fast-growing company, solving complex data problems at scale. We are driven by strong work ethics, high standards of excellence, and a spirit of collaboration. We promote innovation, commitment, and accountability. Our goal is to cultivate a healthy work environment that fosters a sense of belonging, encourages teamwork, and brings out the best in every individual. Why Acceldata? Acceldata is redefining data observability for enterprise data systems. Founded by experts who recognized the need for innovative monitoring and management solutions in a cloud-first, AI-driven environment, our platform empowers data teams to effectively manage data products. We address common challenges such as scaling and performance issues, cost overruns, and data quality problems by providing operational visibility, proactive alerts, and monitoring reliability across the various environments. Delivered as a SaaS product, Acceldata's solutions have been embraced by global customers, such as HPE, HSBC, Visa, Freddie Mac, Manulife, Workday, Zoominfo, GSK, Oracle, PubMatic, PhonePe (Walmart), Hersheys, Dun & Bradstreet, and many more. items.descriptionHtml:
Acceldata is reimagining the way companies observe their Data!

Acceldata is the pioneer and leader in data observability, revolutionizing how enterprises manage and observe data by offering comprehensive insights into various key aspects of data, data pipelines and data infrastructure across various environments. Our platform empowers data teams to manage products effectively by ensuring data quality, preventing failures, and controlling costs.

As a Senior Product Manager

You'll play a key role in setting the near term goals and long term strategy for the whole product. You'll lead collaboration efforts with cross functional teams to deliver product success. You'll build a coherent product narrative that ties together all of the strands of a product or set of features into a coherent whole. You'll be the go-to person for all strategic and tactical concerns on the product and business area and drive decisions to resolution.

A day in the life of Senior Product Manager

  • Work with cross functional teams and key stake holders to develop product vision, roadmap and design.
  • Work with a team of 15+ engineers to plan and execute the product roadmap.
  • Drive the product strategy for the sub-group.
  • Mentor the Product managers by providing deep understanding of the overall product strategy, provide high level insights from formative research, market gaps or cutting edge technical insights.
  • Drive the broader roadmap with a view beyond the next 12 months

You are a great fit for this role if you have

  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related field.
  • 10+ years of experience in Product Development setup, with at least 4+ years of experience in Product Management.
  • 3+ years of Enterprise Data Product experience as a PM.
  • Experience in full cycle Product Management ( Ideation to Launch )- track record of delivering successful products and features, all the way through to adoption
  • Experience leading Cross functional collaboration efforts.
  • Deep technical understanding of the product and the complexities involved in building the product.
  • Strong business acumen.
  • Excellent articulation and presentation skills- ability to clearly and concisely communicate complex topics to diverse stakeholders (engineers, customers, etc.) in written and verbal form.
  • Deep understanding of databases, data processing platforms, analytics tools and machine learning platforms.
  • Ability to think rigorously and make hard decisions and trade-offs.
  • Ability to work in ambiguous, fast-moving environments and have a curiosity to learn the domain at a deep level.
  • Developing a high level of autonomy and responsibility while encouraging a healthy work environment.

We care for our team

  • Mentorship & Growth
  • ESOPs
  • Medical and Life Insurance
  • Paid Maternity & Parental Leave
  • Corporate Uber Program
  • Learning & Development Support

Acceldata for All

We are a fast-growing company, solving complex data problems at scale. We are driven by strong work ethics, high standards of excellence, and a spirit of collaboration. We promote innovation, commitment, and accountability. Our goal is to cultivate a healthy work environment that fosters a sense of belonging, encourages teamwork, and brings out the best in every individual.

 Why Acceldata?

Acceldata is redefining data observability for enterprise data systems. Founded by experts who recognized the need for innovative monitoring and management solutions in a cloud-first, AI-driven environment, our platform empowers data teams to effectively manage data products. We address common challenges such as scaling and performance issues, cost overruns, and data quality problems by providing operational visibility, proactive alerts, and monitoring reliability across the various environments.

Delivered as a SaaS product, Acceldata's solutions have been embraced by global customers, such as HPE, HSBC, Visa, Freddie Mac, Manulife, Workday, Zoominfo, GSK, Oracle, PubMatic, PhonePe (Walmart), Hersheys, Dun & Bradstreet, and many more. Acceldata is a Series-C funded company and its investors include Insight Partners, March Capital, Lightspeed, Sorenson Ventures, Industry Ventures, and Emergent Ventures.

items.title: Senior Product Manager - RPay
items.description: Teamwork makes the stream work. Roku is changing how the world watches TVRoku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers.From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines.  About the Team & Role The Roku Pay, Core Payments team has played a pivotal role in driving Roku’s rapid growth in online subscriptions, powered by a seamless in-streaming, on-TV purchase experience. Supporting a wide range of Roku teams, Core Payments enables transactions across multiple consumer touchpoints — including TV, web, mobile, and advertising. These teams employ diverse strategies to sell digital content, from Subscription Video on Demand (SVOD) and Transactional Video on Demand (TVOD) to Roku-branded devices and shoppable ads directly on the device.Core Payments is now seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the creation of our Payment Machine Learning Platform — a high-impact initiative designed to optimize payment retries, routing, and authorization success rates for our subscription business. This platform will harness AI/ML to make real-time, data-driven payment decisions at scale, directly influencing revenue growth, reducing churn, and enhancing customer satisfaction. In this role, you will operate at the intersection of machine learning, payments infrastructure, and subscription growth, collaborating with engineering, data science, finance, and payment operations to deliver a platform that is both technically sophisticated and mission-critical. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced, highly technical environment, possesses deep knowledge of payments and subscription economics, and can balance strategic vision with hands-on execution. You will own the roadmap, drive flawless delivery, and continuously improve the platform through experimentation and data-driven insights. What You’ll Be Doing Define and Drive the Roadmap: Partner with engineering, ML, and business stakeholders to define a high-impact roadmap for the Payment ML Platform, aligned to Roku’s subscription growth goals.Translate Business Goals into Product Requirements/Feature Guides, Convert business KPIs (e.g., authorization rate uplift, churn reduction) into clear product requirements, success metrics, and technical specifications. Lead AI/ML Product Development: Collaborate with ML engineers and data scientists to design, train, and deploy models for retry prediction, payment routing, and fraud prevention.Champion Experimentation:  Design and oversee A/B and multivariate tests to validate hypotheses, measure incremental lift, and drive continuous improvement.Hands-On Execution: Roll up your sleeves to write PRDs, define acceptance criteria, review model performance dashboards, and troubleshoot issues with engineering.Stakeholder Management: Communicate roadmap progress, risks, and learnings to executives and cross-functional partners.Post-Launch Optimization: Monitor KPIs, identify opportunities for improvement, and prioritize enhancements to maximize impact.Ensure Compliance & Security: Work with legal and compliance teams to meet PCI-DSS, GDPR, and other regulatory requirements for payment data.  We’re Excited If You Have 8+ years in product management, ideally with experience in payments, fintech, subscription businesses, or ML-driven platforms. Proven track record of shipping complex products in partnership with engineering, data science, and operations teams. Strong understanding of payment processing flows, gateways, acquirer optimization, and retry logic. Experience defining and measuring KPIs, running large-scale A/B tests, and making data-driven decisions. Ability to articulate complex ML concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Comfort working in fast-paced, highly iterative environments. Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, or related field; MBA a plus.    Our Hybrid Work ApproachRoku fosters an inclusive and collaborative environment where teams work in the office Monday through Thursday. Fridays are flexible for remote work except for employees whose roles are required to be in the office five days a week or employees who are in offices with a five day in office policy. BenefitsRoku is committed to offering a diverse range of benefits as part of our compensation package to support our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits include global access to mental health and financial wellness support and resources. Local benefits include statutory and voluntary benefits which may include healthcare (medical, dental, and vision), life, accident, disability, commuter, and retirement options (401(k)/pension). Employees are supported in taking time off, in accordance with local leave policies and other personal needs to support their evolving work and life needs. It's important to note that not every benefit is available in all locations or for every role. For details specific to your location, please consult with your recruiter. AccommodationsRoku welcomes applicants of all backgrounds and provides reasonable accommodations and adjustments in accordance with applicable law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any point in the hiring process, please direct your inquiries to EmployeeRelations@Roku.com. The Roku CultureRoku is a great place for people who want to work in a fast-paced environment where everyone is focused on the company's success rather than their own. We try to surround ourselves with people who are great at their jobs, who are easy to work with, and who keep their egos in check. We appreciate a sense of humor. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. In short, at Roku you'll be part of a company that's changing how the world watches TV. We have a unique culture that we are proud of. We think of ourselves primarily as problem-solvers, which itself is a two-part idea. We come up with the solution, but the solution isn't real until it is built and delivered to the customer. That penchant for action gives us a pragmatic approach to innovation, one that has served us well since 2002. To learn more about Roku, our global footprint, and how we've grown, visit https://www.weareroku.com/factsheet.By providing your information, you acknowledge that you want Roku to contact you about job roles, that you have read Roku's Applicant Privacy Notice, and understand that Roku will use your information as described in that notice. If you do not wish to receive any communications from Roku regarding this role or similar roles in the future, you may unsubscribe at any time by emailing WorkforcePrivacy@Roku.com.
items.descriptionHtml:
Teamwork makes the stream work.

 

Roku is changing how the world watches TV

Roku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers.

From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines.

 

About the Team & Role 

The Roku Pay, Core Payments team has played a pivotal role in driving Roku’s rapid growth in online subscriptions, powered by a seamless in-streaming, on-TV purchase experience. Supporting a wide range of Roku teams, Core Payments enables transactions across multiple consumer touchpoints — including TV, web, mobile, and advertising. These teams employ diverse strategies to sell digital content, from Subscription Video on Demand (SVOD) and Transactional Video on Demand (TVOD) to Roku-branded devices and shoppable ads directly on the device.

Core Payments is now seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the creation of our Payment Machine Learning Platform — a high-impact initiative designed to optimize payment retries, routing, and authorization success rates for our subscription business.
This platform will harness AI/ML to make real-time, data-driven payment decisions at scale, directly influencing revenue growth, reducing churn, and enhancing customer satisfaction. In this role, you will operate at the intersection of machine learning, payments infrastructure, and subscription growth, collaborating with engineering, data science, finance, and payment operations to deliver a platform that is both technically sophisticated and mission-critical.
The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced, highly technical environment, possesses deep knowledge of payments and subscription economics, and can balance strategic vision with hands-on execution. You will own the roadmap, drive flawless delivery, and continuously improve the platform through experimentation and data-driven insights. 

What You’ll Be Doing 

  • Define and Drive the Roadmap: Partner with engineering, ML, and business stakeholders to define a high-impact roadmap for the Payment ML Platform, aligned to Roku’s subscription growth goals.
  • Translate Business Goals into Product Requirements/Feature Guides, Convert business KPIs (e.g., authorization rate uplift, churn reduction) into clear product requirements, success metrics, and technical specifications. 
  • Lead AI/ML Product Development: Collaborate with ML engineers and data scientists to design, train, and deploy models for retry prediction, payment routing, and fraud prevention.
  • Champion Experimentation:  Design and oversee A/B and multivariate tests to validate hypotheses, measure incremental lift, and drive continuous improvement.
  • Hands-On Execution: Roll up your sleeves to write PRDs, define acceptance criteria, review model performance dashboards, and troubleshoot issues with engineering.
  • Stakeholder Management: Communicate roadmap progress, risks, and learnings to executives and cross-functional partners.
  • Post-Launch Optimization: Monitor KPIs, identify opportunities for improvement, and prioritize enhancements to maximize impact.
  • Ensure Compliance & Security: Work with legal and compliance teams to meet PCI-DSS, GDPR, and other regulatory requirements for payment data. 

 

We’re Excited If You Have 

  • 8+ years in product management, ideally with experience in payments, fintech, subscription businesses, or ML-driven platforms. 
  • Proven track record of shipping complex products in partnership with engineering, data science, and operations teams. 
  • Strong understanding of payment processing flows, gateways, acquirer optimization, and retry logic. 
  • Experience defining and measuring KPIs, running large-scale A/B tests, and making data-driven decisions. 
  • Ability to articulate complex ML concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. 
  • Comfort working in fast-paced, highly iterative environments. 
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, or related field; MBA a plus. 
 

 

Our Hybrid Work Approach

Roku fosters an inclusive and collaborative environment where teams work in the office Monday through Thursday. Fridays are flexible for remote work except for employees whose roles are required to be in the office five days a week or employees who are in offices with a five day in office policy.

 

Benefits

Roku is committed to offering a diverse range of benefits as part of our compensation package to support our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits include global access to mental health and financial wellness support and resources. Local benefits include statutory and voluntary benefits which may include healthcare (medical, dental, and vision), life, accident, disability, commuter, and retirement options (401(k)/pension). Employees are supported in taking time off, in accordance with local leave policies and other personal needs to support their evolving work and life needs. It's important to note that not every benefit is available in all locations or for every role. For details specific to your location, please consult with your recruiter.

 

Accommodations

Roku welcomes applicants of all backgrounds and provides reasonable accommodations and adjustments in accordance with applicable law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any point in the hiring process, please direct your inquiries to EmployeeRelations@Roku.com.

 

The Roku Culture

Roku is a great place for people who want to work in a fast-paced environment where everyone is focused on the company's success rather than their own. We try to surround ourselves with people who are great at their jobs, who are easy to work with, and who keep their egos in check. We appreciate a sense of humor. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. In short, at Roku you'll be part of a company that's changing how the world watches TV. 

We have a unique culture that we are proud of. We think of ourselves primarily as problem-solvers, which itself is a two-part idea. We come up with the solution, but the solution isn't real until it is built and delivered to the customer. That penchant for action gives us a pragmatic approach to innovation, one that has served us well since 2002. 

To learn more about Roku, our global footprint, and how we've grown, visit https://www.weareroku.com/factsheet.

By providing your information, you acknowledge that you want Roku to contact you about job roles, that you have read Roku's Applicant Privacy Notice, and understand that Roku will use your information as described in that notice. If you do not wish to receive any communications from Roku regarding this role or similar roles in the future, you may unsubscribe at any time by emailing WorkforcePrivacy@Roku.com.

items.title: Product Manager (B2C)
