Precisely is an AI-first organization. All employees are expected to demonstrate proficiency in applying AI tools to accelerate their work, improve output quality, and eliminate low-value tasks. Candidates should be comfortable using generative AI tools (e.g., Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT) in their day-to-day workflows, able to evaluate AI-generated outputs critically, and open to continuously adopting new AI capabilities as they emerge.

 

Overview:

Join our Product Management team and become a driving force behind the Master Data Management Product (MDM) group. In this pivotal role, you'll delve into market trends across our existing and target customer segments, transforming insights into actionable business requirements, functional use cases, and compelling product solutions involving AI. Collaborating closely with cross-functional teams, you'll emerge as the subject matter expert on MDM, guiding product development and supporting sales with your deep product knowledge and market insights. Your expertise will identify growth opportunities, inform strategy, and ensure our products continue to meet the evolving needs of our market.

What you will do:

  • Manage Product Requirements: Gather and prioritize product needs from customers, sales, support, and other teams. Create clear documentation (MRDs and PRDs), and work with Engineering to build the roadmaps.
  • Develop Business Plans: Collaborate and contribute to create long-term business plans for selected market segments. Track performance and update stakeholders regularly.
  • Build Business Cases: Support product decisions, use of AI, and partnerships with solid business models and justifications.
  • Communicate Effectively: Share updates clearly through documents, emails, and presentations. Ensure stakeholders understand market trends and product direction.
  • Provide Leadership: Be the voice of the customer and influence the direction of the product.
  • Collaborate Across Teams: Work closely with Marketing, Engineering, Strategic Planning, and other product teams to make informed decisions and expand product influence.
  • Track Success Metrics: Monitor and report on key performance indicators to ensure goals are being met.

What we are looking for:

  • Experience: 8+ years of product management experience building data products, shared services, or internal-facing capabilities used by multiple product teams.
  • Education: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. Background in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or related fields is helpful but not required.
  • Product Management Expertise: Demonstrated ability to design and deliver solutions specifically in product information management or other data domains—such as material master, customer master, reference data, etc.
  • Experience with a broad spectrum of data management practices, including data quality, data integration, data governance, and metadata management.
  • Experience with AI: Demonstrated expertise in justifying and embedding AI in products.
  • Hybrid Cloud Expertise: Experience with hybrid cloud deployments and SaaS solutions, particularly in AWS.
  • Strategic & Execution Skills: Proven ability to define platform standards, manage complex stakeholder groups, and drive execution in an Agile environment with multiple dependent teams and a high-volume feature backlog.
  • Conceptual Technical Proficiency: Comfortable partnering with engineering on high-level architectural decisions involving APIs, integrations, platform services, and hybrid deployment models.
  • Tools & Methodology Experience: Hands-on experience with Agile development practices and product tools such as Aha!. Demonstrated ability to influence without authority and lead cross-functional initiatives across product lines.
  • Enthusiasm to work in a fast-paced, demanding business environment while collaborating effectively with cross-functional groups, management, sales and customers.
  • Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage high volumes of work
  • Excellent analytical, interpersonal, managerial, and organizational skills

The personal data that you provide as a part of this job application will be handled in accordance with relevant laws. For more information about how Precisely handles the personal data of job applicants, please see the Precisely Candidate Privacy Notice

items.descriptionHtml:
Job Profile: Product Manager I

Location: Bangalore | Karnataka

Years of Experience: 3-5 years in Product Management.

About The Team & Role

Swiggy’s Dineout team builds and scales products that power India’s dining-out experience , from restaurant discovery and reservations to payments and offers. Integrated within the Swiggy ecosystem, the team focuses on driving user engagement, partner success, and business growth through data-driven product innovation.

Product Manager I works with various internal product teams, UX researchers/designers, data scientists, analytics, marketing and business to build a strategy and roadmap for their respective charter helping our power users find value and convenience in the most efficient manner leading to continued customer delight and seamless experiences.

What will you get to do here?

>Establish & own long term vision for several products & platforms at Swiggy while strategizing , planning and influencing to deliver on the planned roadmap

>Partnering with business, operations, product, marketing, supply and Analytics teams to deliver on the roadmap while unlocking long term value for our customers and internal stakeholders.

>Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define strategic roadmaps across Swiggy's focus areas based on the AOP targets and execute as per the aligned roadmaps

>Build products for the stable SDO business and 0 to 1 business building.

>Collaborate with product teams across the org to ideate and find win-win-win solutions on common objectives’ across multiple categories and product lines impacted

>Manage, facilitate team members at all levels to design and build differentiated experience for Foster a culture of innovation & learning with high emphasis on sustainable rituals

>Determine the targets / success metrics for the org based on the organizational strategy and be the custodian for these metrics.

What qualities are we looking for?

>Minimum 3 years of work experience in Product Management with extensive experience in user research, consumer backward thinking, design thinking and building consumer experiences in the e-commerce/ consumer internet industry.

>Must have a deep understanding of the broader ecommerce/consumer internet ecosystem, new technologies and their applications to improve payment experience.

>Proven track record of building and shipping high-quality data products in large internet companies impacting millions of users.

>Ability to work with third party solution providers, such as PoS partners, Payment Gateways, and other eco-system enabling companies within the Dining out industry.

>You are consumer obsessed, but you can walk a tightrope balancing consumer delight with potentially orthogonal org goals.

>Well versed with running experiments & is considered an expert in analyzing data, showcasing a deep understanding of the 360-degree interaction effects and potential long-term impacts, can cull out the unknowns and hedge against them.

>Ability to work under pressure and deliver tangible business impact in time-critical situations

>Highly customer-obsessed thinking with a deep understanding of customer needs and aspirations

>AI-native thinking in terms of leveraging AI tools as partners from ideation to release

We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by the law.

Join us in our mission to help customers accelerate business transformation with simple, powerful, and secure solutions.The ideal candidate thrives in an innovative, fast-paced environment and is collaborative, accountable, ready, and empathetic. We’re looking for individuals who believe they can accomplish more as a team and create lasting growth for themselves and others. We hire based on attitude, competency, and commitment. Solarians are ready to advance our world-class solutions in a fast-paced environment and accept the challenge to lead with purpose. If you’re looking to build your career with an exceptional team, you’ve come to the right place. Join SolarWinds and grow with us!  Senior Product Manager - Intelligent Events, Health, and SLOsRole Summary: This Senior Product Manager will own the strategy for how SolarWinds defines, measures, and communicates the reliability and operational health of customer systems.  The core focus is transforming raw observability data into actionable, intelligent events and proactive health scores, directly enabling customer Service Level Objective (SLO) management and incident reduction.  This role drives the product vision for event correlation and anomaly detection, ensuring our platform moves customers from reactive monitoring to proactive incident prevention.  Success will be measured by the adoption of SLO features, the reduction in alert volume for customers, and the quality of system health scoring.Core Responsibilities: Define the product strategy and roadmap for Service Level Indicators (SLIs) and Service Level Objectives (SLOs), ensuring seamless configuration, tracking, and reporting for our enterprise users.  Lead the development of the event management platform, focusing on advanced correlation, root cause analysis, and proprietary noise reduction techniques to combat alert fatigue.  Establish a unified, platform-wide health and risk scoring model that synthesizes metrics, logs, and traces into a single, understandable view of system wellness.  Collaborate with machine learning and engineering teams to integrate AI-driven anomaly detection and predictive alerting features for the proactive identification of impending issues.  Must be the voice of the Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) persona, translating industry best practices into powerful, simple-to-use product features.Required Qualifications: Deep domain expertise in AIOps, event management, items.descriptionHtml:
At SolarWinds, we’re a people-first company. Our purpose is to enrich the lives of the people we serve—including our employees, customers, shareholders, partners, and communities. Join us in our mission to help customers accelerate business transformation with simple, powerful, and secure solutions.

The ideal candidate thrives in an innovative, fast-paced environment and is collaborative, accountable, ready, and empathetic. We’re looking for individuals who believe they can accomplish more as a team and create lasting growth for themselves and others. We hire based on attitude, competency, and commitment. Solarians are ready to advance our world-class solutions in a fast-paced environment and accept the challenge to lead with purpose. If you’re looking to build your career with an exceptional team, you’ve come to the right place. Join SolarWinds and grow with us!

 

Senior Product Manager - Intelligent Events, Health, and SLOs


Role Summary:

This Senior Product Manager will own the strategy for how SolarWinds defines, measures, and communicates the reliability and operational health of customer systems.  The core focus is transforming raw observability data into actionable, intelligent events and proactive health scores, directly enabling customer Service Level Objective (SLO) management and incident reduction.  This role drives the product vision for event correlation and anomaly detection, ensuring our platform moves customers from reactive monitoring to proactive incident prevention.  Success will be measured by the adoption of SLO features, the reduction in alert volume for customers, and the quality of system health scoring.


Core Responsibilities:

Define the product strategy and roadmap for Service Level Indicators (SLIs) and Service Level Objectives (SLOs), ensuring seamless configuration, tracking, and reporting for our enterprise users.  Lead the development of the event management platform, focusing on advanced correlation, root cause analysis, and proprietary noise reduction techniques to combat alert fatigue.  Establish a unified, platform-wide health and risk scoring model that synthesizes metrics, logs, and traces into a single, understandable view of system wellness.  Collaborate with machine learning and engineering teams to integrate AI-driven anomaly detection and predictive alerting features for the proactive identification of impending issues.  Must be the voice of the Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) persona, translating industry best practices into powerful, simple-to-use product features.


Required Qualifications:

Deep domain expertise in AIOps, event management, and Service Reliability Engineering (SRE) practices, with specific experience in defining and launching SLO/SLI features.  Proven experience in product managing technical platforms that leverage data science, machine learning, or statistical models for event correlation and anomaly detection.  Strong working knowledge of how observability data (metrics, logs, traces) is used to calculate system health and drive automated workflows.  Must possess impeccable oral and written communications skills, exceptional leadership capabilities to drive cross-functional alignment, and proven expertise in product strategy and product management fundamentals.  Demonstrated ability to deliver features that fundamentally improve a customer’s operational efficiency and reduce Mean Time to Repair (MTTR).

 

SolarWinds is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. SolarWinds will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, disability, veteran status or any other characteristic protected by law.

All applications are treated in accordance with the SolarWinds Privacy Notice: https://www.solarwinds.com/applicant-privacy-notice

items.descriptionHtml:
Company Background


zenda is a specialist financial technology partner to schools, colleges & nurseries, building products & services exclusively for the education sector.


It is a friendly app for families that helps simplify the discovery and payment of tuition and education related services, with flexibility. On the other side, It helps institutions with enabling end-to-end digitization and improving cash-flow predictability. zenda's innovative technology stack integrates deeply with the platforms used in the educational sector thereby eliminating manual efforts/reconciliations.


We are expanding our teams aggressively across all functions in the UAE and India are looking for talent professionals with an entrepreneurial mindset and the passion to create disruptive businesses.


Product Manager

Location: Bangalore

Experience Level: 5+ Years


Key Responsibilities


  • Lead product strategy and execution, driving high-impact outcomes and accelerating delivery in a fast-paced environment.
  • Own and continuously improve the mobile-first user experience, with a strong focus on usability, engagement, and performance for B2C users.
  • Translate business needs into clear product requirements, specifications, and user stories for engineering, design, and QA teams.
  • Collaborate closely with design, engineering, operations, and business stakeholders to define product vision, strategy, and long-term roadmap.
  • Drive roadmap planning, prioritization, execution, and delivery aligned with organizational goals.
  • Leverage design thinking and product intuition to create intuitive, user-centric digital experiences that solve real customer problems.
  • Work closely with partners and customers to ensure seamless integrations, reliable product performance, and an excellent end-user experience.
  • Identify opportunities to thoughtfully integrate AI-enabled features where they can meaningfully enhance product functionality, personalization, or efficiency.


Requirements


  • 5+ years of overall experience across product, technology, or design roles, with at least 3 years of product management experience building digital products or mobile apps.
  • Strong background in UX/UI or product design (ex-designer preferred) with the ability to think deeply about user journeys and product usability.
  • Proven experience building B2C products, with a strong understanding of user engagement and retention.
  • Hands-on experience working closely with engineering teams to ship production-grade products and features.
  • Experience owning mobile app products (iOS/Android) and optimizing mobile-first user experiences.
  • Familiarity with AI-enabled product capabilities such as chat interfaces, personalization, or automation is a plus.
  • Excellent communication skills — both written and verbal — with strong attention to detail.
  • Entrepreneurial or startup mindset — comfortable with ambiguity, fast iteration, and building products from 0→1 and 1→n.
  • Prior exposure to billing, invoicing, accounting, or financial workflow modules is a plus.

items.descriptionHtml:
Job Description

Our team is at the heart of building and improving tooling that supports all aspects of Chase offering including new market expansion. We are looking for solution-oriented, commercially minded, customer-focused product managers, used to working in an agile environment who want to be a part of building exceptional user experience within a diverse and inclusive team.

As a Product Manager - Vice President at JPMorganChase within the International Consumer Bank, you are an integral part of the team that innovates new product offerings and leads the end-to-end product life cycle. As a core leader, you are responsible for acting as the voice of the customer and developing profitable products that provide customer value. Utilizing your deep understanding of how to get a product off the ground, you guide the successful launch of products, gather crucial feedback, and ensure top-tier client experiences. With a strong commitment to scalability, resiliency, and stability, you collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality products that exceed customer expectations.

You will lead the development, testing, implementation and maintenance of CRM platform to enable delivery of industry-leading customer service experience.

Job Responsibilities

  • Define the roadmap and objectives for our customer service operations technology platform
  • Take responsibility for the successful delivery of the product, ensuring that it meets the acceptance criteria of your stakeholders
  • Partner with the Tech Lead to ensure alignment and feasibility of product, technical vision, and requirements
  • Create testing and implementation plans
  • Ensure the product meets all legal, regulatory, risk, and control requirements
  • Be hands-on, detail-oriented, and outcome-focused as well as a strong team player

Required Qualifications, Capabilities And Skills

  • Demonstrate expertise in delivering and scaling customer service operation technology products
  • Have experience in a complex software environment with Agile/Scrum development
    • Show strong experience with planning, prioritizing, and maintaining product backlogs
    • Demonstrate strong experience assessing value, developing cases, and prioritizing stories, epics, and themes to ensure work focuses on those with maximum value and is aligned with product strategy
    • Possess expert-level knowledge in Agile principles, processes, and best practices
    • Exhibit strong experience with hypothesis-driven development, product experimentation, and product analytics
  • Communicate strongly and feel comfortable explaining design, architecture, and roadmap to a wide range of audiences from both technology and business backgrounds
Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities And Skills

  • Good understanding of process mapping
  • Experience with Customer Relationship Management and Operations or Customer Service
  • Knowledge of Salesforce out of the box functionality and tools

ABOUT US

JPMorganChase, one of the oldest financial institutions, offers innovative financial solutions to millions of consumers, small businesses and many of the world’s most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands. Our history spans over 200 years and today we are a leader in investment banking, consumer and small business banking, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management.

We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.

About The Team

Our professionals in our Corporate Functions cover a diverse range of areas from finance and risk to human resources and marketing. Our corporate teams are an essential part of our company, ensuring that we’re setting our businesses, clients, customers and employees up for success.

The Digital team is dedicated to creating innovative, industry-leading products and experiences that help customers access, share and control their financial data so they can make smart decisions with their money. Teams enable innovation while adhering to the firm’s data sharing principles of security, customer control and convenience, and privacy.

Precisely's 2500 employees are unified by four company core values that are central to who we are and how we operate: Openness, Determination, Individuality, and Collaboration. We are committed to career development for our employees and offer opportunities for growth, learning and building community. With a "work from anywhere" culture, we celebrate diversity in a distributed environment with a presence in 30 countries as well as 20 offices in over 5 continents. Learn more about why it's an exciting time to join Precisely!

Overview:

Join our Data Integration team and take ownership of the Connect CDC product line. You will translate market trends and customer insights into a clear product roadmap. You will work closely with engineering, design, and go-to-market teams to deliver high-quality solutions. As the subject matter expert on Connect CDC, you will guide product development and identify growth opportunities. You will ensure the product continues to meet the evolving demands of our 12,000 customers worldwide. This is an individual contributor role with significant impact and visibility.

What you will do:

  • Define and own the Connect CDC roadmap, prioritizing capabilities that address customer needs and drive competitive differentiation in data integration and Change Data Capture.
  • Conduct ongoing research with customers, prospects, and analysts to surface unmet needs and inform product direction.
  • Author clear business requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria; maintain a well-prioritized backlog in close collaboration with engineering.
  • Partner with Engineering, UX, Support, and GTM teams throughout the product lifecycle to ensure on-time, high-quality delivery.
  • Monitor the CDC and data integration landscape and develop a working understanding of technical concepts such as connectors, pipelines, replication, and cloud/hybrid deployment.
  • Align product direction with internal stakeholders across Product, Sales, and Customer Success; serve as the go-to resource on Connect CDC capabilities and strategy.

What we are looking for:

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. A background in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field is helpful but not required.
  • Experience: 5–7 years of product management experience building SaaS platforms, shared services, or internal-facing capabilities used by multiple product teams.
  • Demonstrated ability to design and deliver reusable platform components—such as entitlement management, usage metering, tenant administration, and API-driven services—that support cross-team development at scale.
  • Proven ability to define platform standards, manage complex stakeholder groups, and drive execution in an Agile environment with multiple dependent teams.
  • Hands-on experience with Agile development practices and product tools such as Aha! or similar roadmapping and backlog management platforms.
  • Familiarity with data integration concepts such as CDC, ETL/ELT, connectors, or real-time data pipelines.
  • Travel is required: No.
  • Bonus points for: experience with hybrid cloud deployments (particularly AWS and MongoDB), hands-on familiarity with Kubernetes or API integration frameworks, exposure to CDC technologies, background in data quality or data governance, or familiarity with SQL, databases, or streaming platforms such as Kafka.

The personal data that you provide as a part of this job application will be handled in accordance with relevant laws. For more information about how Precisely handles the personal data of job applicants, please see the Precisely Candidate Privacy Notice

items.descriptionHtml:
A career that’s the whole package!

At Conga, we’ve built a community where our colleagues can thrive. Here you’ll find opportunities to innovate and support growth through individual and team development, all within an environment where every voice is heard.

Conga lines up commercial operations so companies run as connected, smarter businesses. By unifying the people and processes that drive commerce, Conga aligns pricing, quoting, contracting, rebates, and communications so teams stay in sync and buyers keep moving forward. The result is trusted decisions, consistent buyer experiences, and profitable growth. More than 10,000 customers worldwide, including over 50% of the Fortune 100®, trust Conga when commercial complexity is high and global impact is on the line.

Our approach is grounded in the Conga Way, a framework that reflects our values and drives everything from hiring to decision-making, as well as key programs including recognition. Created with direct input from our colleagues, the Conga Way forms the foundation of our vibrant culture.

Job Title: Principal Product Manager

Locations: Bangalore

Reports to: Director, Product Management

A quick snapshot…

The Principal Product Manager at Conga is responsible for the product direction, planning, execution, and launch of product releases. Conga thrives in solving our customer’s business problems and creating raving fans in the process. Here you bridge the technical and business worlds as you design products that our customers and their customers love! Join the team at Conga to help drive the next phase of growth.

Why it’s a big deal…

A Conga Principal Product Manager is continuously adapting & is excited to work on Products, cross-functionally to guide products from conception to launch by connecting the technical and business worlds. You have a technical passion to break down complex problems into steps that drive the product development lifecycle including analyzing, positioning, packaging, promoting & tailoring our solutions to meet our customer needs.

Are you the person we’re looking for?

Relevant Experience. You should have 12+ years of overall experience and 3-5 years in building B2B SAS products from concept to post-launch, API, and integrations & familiarity with building ML based products. Ability and confidence to work closely with select customers in pre-sales and collaborative scenarios. Experience with integrated solutions for major platforms such as Microsoft, SAP, Oracle, or Salesforce is strongly desired. You’ll be involved in technical publications, providing guidance to product education teams for technical and product documentation, and transitioning finished projects to the Customer-facing teams.

Research and Creativity. Own the research, action plans, adoption plans, and results for the products. Map business ideas and use cases into product requirements and roadmap, set goals, determine priorities, and track deliverables. You will create solutions to problems with our products while always focusing on the best user experience and balancing company goals, product cohesion, and ease of implementation, research, wireframing, and incorporating user feedback into the product.

Leadership skills. Work across multiple teams to deliver quality software at a consistent pace, which aligns to the product vision. Guide the scrum teams to deliver great software at an impressive pace. Keep the team aligned with the work at hand and the completion of the greater feature, gather product requirements from stakeholders, manage release status for development and communicate status to other departments in the company. Work closely with the development team to decompose high-level features into user stories, quickly provide just-in-time decisions to system-level questions, and defect triage.

Analyze and prioritize. Schedule development work for releases. Work with product management and development teams to create and maintain product priorities, track schedules against delivery commitments, and escalate risks and issues. Work with and maintain communication with stakeholders. Analyze product metrics to understand product performance.

Customer experience. Aligning customer experience strategies with marketing initiatives and informing customers about new product features and functionalities. Analyzing customer feedback on product ranges and new releases and preparing reports accordingly. Documenting processes and logging technical issues, as well as customer compliments and complaints.

Education: A Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or equivalent

Here’s What Will Give You An Edge…

 Champion the Customer. You understand that customers are one of the business’s most important assets and you take that seriously. With more than 11,000 customers around the globe entrusting Conga with their business we make a commitment to their success and would love to hear how you will too!

Natural collaborator. You work effectively with Product Manager, are proficient with Agile and a collaborative cross-functional approach to building awesome software. You are comfortable working with teams and collaborating on best practices across multiple Agile teams. You constantly seek opinions and solicit feedback to create the best work possible. You don’t know any other way. It’s a team effort and you completely appreciate that.

Passion. We know it when we see it. Passion is not about saying how much you love what you do in your most excited voice. Passion is revealed in your truest self. It’s about what you’ve accomplished; how you want to grow; the ideas you have; your philosophies. It’s demonstrating through your words and your actions that you truly believe in what you do -- and where you work. That it matters to you. And that’s pretty cool. 

Did we pique your interest? 

If this sounds like the kind of job you would love in the kind of environment where you would thrive, please click apply. We'd love to hear from you! 

Preferred Resume Format

We accept resumes in any format, we suggest using PDF or plain text. These formats help ensure that your resume’s formatting remains intact, making it easier for our recruiters to review your application promptly.

Don’t meet every requirement for the role?  

Studies have shown that women and members of ethnic minorities are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single qualification. At Conga we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace, so if you’re excited about this role but your experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You just might be the right candidate for this or other roles. 

Additional Information

Conga is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, age, national origin, disability, parental or pregnancy status, marriage and civil partnership, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We understand interviewing can be stressful for those with disabilities. If reasonable accommodation is needed to allow you to show us your best self, please let your recruiter know as soon as possible. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines. Conga is not open to third party solicitation or resumes for our posted FTE positions. Resumes received from third party agencies that are unsolicited will be considered complimentary.

Conga's Applicant Privacy Statement

The information you provide during the application process will be used in accordance with Conga’s Applicant Privacy Statement (https://conga.com/applicant-privacy-statement). By submitting your application, you acknowledge and agree to the terms outlined in this privacy statement.

items.descriptionHtml:
Company Description

Finnable is one of India’s fastest-growing lending platforms, offering personal loans of up to ₹10 lakh through a fully digital process for salaried professionals across 170+ cities. Founded by fintech experts Nitin Gupta and Amit Arora, Finnable combines advanced financial technology and risk management to provide responsible and scalable credit solutions. Recently, Finnable closed a ₹250 crore Pre-Series C funding round and received recognition from Forbes, ET BFSI, and the Great Place To Work certification. With a strong commitment to financial inclusion, Finnable focuses on building fast, fair, and accessible credit solutions for salaried India.


Role Overview

This role requires an understanding of Micro & Small Enterprises in Teir 2/3 geographies, knowledge of their cash flow nuances, ability to design and scale innovative credit products tailored to those segments.

The ideal candidate will bridge on-ground insights (including competitive benchmarking) with product, credit, and technology to drive growth, improve customer experience, and unlock new revenue streams.


Key Responsibilities

1. Product Strategy & Development

  • Design customer-centric loan products based on local market needs and income patterns
  • Continuously innovate on product constructs (Technology, TAT, Tenure, Ticket size, Repayment models, Hybrid income assessment, Type of Security etc.)


2. Customer & Market Understanding

  • Spend significant time in the field to understand customer behavior, needs, and pain points
  • Build strong insights into MSME cash flows, salaried informal segments, and local business ecosystems
  • Translate ground-level insights into scalable product features and policies


3. Financial Needs Analysis

  • Analyze customer financial requirements across lifecycle stages (working capital, expansion, personal needs)
  • Develop frameworks for income assessment in low-documentation/alternate sources environments
  • Work closely with credit teams to refine underwriting models for these segments


4. Cross-Sell & Revenue Enhancement

  • Identify and drive cross-sell opportunities (PL, Mobile, Vehicle Gold, Cards etc.)
  • Design bundled offerings tailored to customer segments
  • Improve customer lifetime value (LTV) through product innovation


5. Process Optimization (Lead to Disbursement)

  • Own end-to-end funnel: lead generation → login → approval → disbursement
  • Identify bottlenecks and improve TAT, conversion rates, and customer experience
  • Collaborate with sales, credit, operations, and collections teams


6. Tech & AI Enablement

  • Partner with tech teams to digitize and streamline processes
  • Leverage AI/ML for Lead scoring, Credit underwriting, Fraud detection, Customer segmentation
  • Drive adoption of digital tools in field operations


7. Stakeholder Management

  • Work cross-functionally with Sales, Credit, Risk, Ops, Tech, and Analytics
  • Align internal teams to deliver product outcomes


Key Requirements

Experience

  • 3–8 years of experience in NBFC/Banking/Fintech
  • Significant exposure to LAP, MSME lending, or retail secured lending
  • Experience working in Tier 2/3/4 markets preferred


Skills & Competencies

  • Field Orientation: Willingness to travel extensively and engage directly with customers
  • Comfort with Ambiguity: Ability to operate in evolving, unstructured environments
  • Customer Insight: Deep understanding of MSME and salaried segments in semi-urban/rural markets
  • Analytical Thinking: Ability to interpret financials, cash flows, and informal income patterns
  • Product Mindset: Strong problem-solving and innovation capabilities
  • Tech Awareness: Understanding of digital lending, AI/ML applications in financial services
  • Execution Focus: Ability to drive outcomes across multiple stakeholders
  • Local Language: Knowing any one or more of Tamil, Kannada and/or Telugu is preferred


Education

  • MBA/PGDM (preferred) or equivalent experience in financial services


Success Metrics

  • Growth in LAP portfolio (disbursements & AUM)
  • Improvement in lead-to-disbursement conversion rates
  • Reduction in TAT across funnel stages
  • Increase in cross-sell revenue
  • Product adoption and customer satisfaction in target segments
